target_audience.primary_persona: Solo travelers who plan and manage their own trips, typically aged 25-45, tech-savvy, safety-conscious, and seeking adventure while maintaining independence
secondary_personas: Backpackers and budget travelers seeking community connections
Digital nomads who need to manage long-term travel itineraries
First-time solo travelers who need extra safety features and guidance
pain_points: Planning trips alone is overwhelming and time-consuming without recommendations
Safety concerns when traveling solo without trusted contacts knowing your location
Difficulty finding destinations, accommodations, and activities suitable for solo travelers
Limited ability to connect with other solo travelers for tips, meetups, or companionship
Managing multiple bookings, itineraries, and travel documents across different platforms
Lack of offline access to travel plans when internet is unavailable
Difficulty documenting and sharing travel experiences in one place
goals: Plan comprehensive multi-day trips with itineraries, bookings, and logistics
Discover destinations and activities popular among solo travelers
Stay safe by sharing location and status with trusted contacts
Connect with other solo travelers for advice, companionship, or meetups
Document travel experiences through journals, photos, and memories
Access travel plans and information offline during trips
Manage all travel-related information in one secure, mobile-first application
target_audience.usage_context: Used throughout the travel lifecycle: pre-trip for planning and discovery, during travel for navigation, safety, and journaling, and post-trip for sharing memories. Primarily mobile-first usage during travel, with some planning activities potentially on tablets. Used in various connectivity scenarios from offline to high-bandwidth environments.

product_vision.one_liner: A comprehensive mobile companion app that empowers solo travelers to plan trips safely, discover destinations, connect with fellow travelers, and document their adventures with confidence.
product_vision.problem_statement: Solo travelers face unique challenges including safety concerns, difficulty planning trips without companion input, limited access to solo-travel-specific recommendations, and lack of community connection during their journeys.
product_vision.value_proposition: SoloAdventurer combines trip planning, destination discovery, safety features, and community connections into one mobile-first platform specifically designed for solo travelers, leveraging AI-powered recommendations and offline capabilities to support independent adventurers.
success_metrics: User engagement: Daily active users (DAU) and session duration during travel periods
Trip completion rate: Percentage of planned trips that are executed and tracked
Safety feature usage: Frequency of location sharing and safety check-in usage
Community engagement: Messages sent, connections made, and meetups organized
Adoption rate: New user registrations and first trip completion within 30 days
App stability: Crash-free sessions and API response times
Offline functionality usage: Percentage of features accessed without internet connection

existing_features: Authentication system with AWS Cognito (login, registration, password reset)
Clean architecture foundation with feature-first organization
Secure token management and session handling
Performance monitoring and error handling infrastructure
Travel feature module with domain models (location updates, travel notes, trip planning)
Integration with AWS CloudWatch for logging
known_gaps: Trip planning UI and full workflow not implemented
Travel community features not built (chat, forums, meetups)
Travel journal functionality incomplete (photo uploads, note editing)
Google Maps integration not fully utilized in UI
Offline data sync and persistence not implemented
Safety features incomplete (emergency contacts, location sharing UI)
Social features for connecting with other travelers missing
Travel documentation storage and management not implemented
technical_debt: TODO comments indicate incomplete implementations in location updates, trip planning, and travel notes
Error tracking service integration incomplete (Sentry/Firebase Crashlytics commented out)
Refresh token handling partially implemented (marked as TODO)
Operation queue persistence not implemented (marked as TODO)
Analytics tracking incomplete (marked as TODO in route guard)
Test coverage needs verification (test structure exists but coverage unknown)
Token refresh logic needs retry mechanism implementation
Some authentication flows may need additional error handling

alternatives: Google Trips (discontinued but set expectations for itinerary management)
Travel Facebook groups and Reddit communities (for connections)
Google Maps and offline maps apps (for navigation)
Instagram and travel blogs (for inspiration and journaling)
Booking platforms like Booking.com and Airbnb (for accommodations)
Travel apps like Roadtrippers and Sygic Travel
differentiators: Solo-travel-first design rather than general travel planning
Integrated safety features specifically for solo travelers
Community features focused on solo traveler connections
AI-powered recommendations for solo-suitable destinations and activities
Offline-first architecture for reliability during travel
All-in-one platform combining planning, safety, community, and journaling
Clean architecture ensuring scalability and maintainability
competitive_context.market_position: Specialized mobile app targeting the underserved solo travel market with a comprehensive approach combining planning, safety, and community - positioned between general travel apps (TripIt, Roadtrippers) and social platforms (Facebook travel groups, Instagram), with unique safety-first features.

technical: Flutter/Dart ecosystem for cross-platform mobile development (iOS/Android)
AWS Cognito dependency for authentication limits flexibility
Supabase as backend database requires internet sync strategy
Google Maps API quota and pricing constraints
Offline functionality requirements add complexity to data synchronization
Clean architecture pattern requires more boilerplate and discipline
resources: Solo developer (Anthony Foran) - limited bandwidth for parallel development
No dedicated QA team - relies on automated testing
Bootstrap/self-funded project - budget constraints for API services
Part-time development effort - slower delivery velocity
No dedicated designer - UI/UX relies on developer and Flutter Material Design
dependencies: AWS Cognito for authentication (user pools, identity pools)
Supabase for backend database and real-time features
Google Maps API for mapping and location services
AWS CloudWatch for logging and monitoring
Third-party APIs for travel data (destinations, accommodations, activities) - not yet integrated
